108.status: Threw bullpen sessions April 14 and April 17 before facing hitters for the first time April 20. He threw 30 pitches over two simulated innings and will continue to ramp up his pitch count.
Progressing well in throwing program. Faced hitters for the first time on April 14 after progressing to throwing full bullpens, and is slated to face hitters again soon.
Made a rehab start in a Minor League game in Arizona but exited with hamstring discomfort after throwing two pitches on April 13. He's progressed to playing catch without any issues.
Right middle fingernail fell off after March 17 start. Threw to hitters on April 11 and “came out really good," per manager Kurt Suzuki, and his fingernail issue has improved substantially.

Started his rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on April 17 and allowed one run in one inning. He said he felt good but is still looking to regain weight and velocity so he'll need a few more appearances.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Played 11 rehab games for Triple-A Norfolk from March 27-April 12 after right hamate surgery. Began new rehab assignment with High-A Frederick on April 18, but will return to Norfolk on April 21.
Getting second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing. Went on IL five days later before transfer to 60-day IL.
Allowed four runs in 3 2/3 innings with five strikeouts and one walk over four rehab outings for Triple-A Norfolk from April 1-17. Assignment was briefly paused for paternity leave.
Struck out two batters in one scoreless inning to begin rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk on April 15. Allowed one run in 1 1/3 innings on April 18.
Hospitalized in Pittsburgh during April 3-5 series. Received IV antibiotics for swelling. Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk on April 18, striking out two in scoreless inning.
MRI showed encouraging results. Not expected to miss extended time. Ran, hit and caught bullpen session in Cleveland on April 17. Encouraged by recovery progress thus far.
Missed April 10 and 11 games vs. Giants after dealing with extreme dehydration, which caused him to faint on April 9 off-day. Took batting practice and ran in Cleveland on April 17.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging in Baltimore on March 23. Running and participating in baseball activities as of April 14.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Swinging a bat as of April 1. Scheduled to begin throwing progression week of April 6.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Exited start on April 20 in the third inning after feeling hamstring grab. Will undergo an MRI on April 21.
Recalled from his Minor League rehab assignment due to discomfort in biceps area. No imaging scheduled at this point.
Recently underwent imaging on his right elbow. Results will determine how soon he can resume Minors assignment.
Resumed playing catch on April 17 and 18. Will likely need short rehab assignment before returning.

111.injury: Ruptured left patellar tendon/left abdominal strain
111.status: Rehab from left knee surgery went well, but now has left abdominal strain that will prolong return to action.
Consultation with Dr. Keith Meister revealed right flexor strain. Shut down from throwing for six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 14.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Last pitched April 12 against the Pirates. Felt discomfort in lower back/side while doing conditioning/running after bullpen session on April 17. Getting treatment daily as Cubs assess next steps.
Did not recover well after each of the past two outings (April 3 and April 8). Still in treatment phase with goal of potentially resuming throwing later this week, per manager Craig Counsell.
Last pitched on April 7. Threw a "diagnostic" bullpen session on April 18 and everything went well, per manager Craig Counsell. Scheduled to face hitters in live BP on April 21.
Threw 37 pitches in three scoreless innings in first rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on April 18. Had two strikeouts, one walk and one hit allowed.
Threw 64 pitches in 3 2/3 innings in a rehab start with Triple-A Iowa on April 16. Reported feeling good on April 17. On target for activation on April 22 to start vs. Phillies.

Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Horton's timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months, per manager Craig Counsell. Horton was transferred to the 60-day IL on April 18.
Shut down from throwing on March 8. Resumed playing catch on April 4. Had a setback and will have elbow surgery on April 20, per manager Craig Counsell.
Injured hand in an accident while working out on April 13. Should be able to return around eligibility date.
Wiggins (Pipeline's No. 1 Cubs prospect and No. 52 on the Top 100) last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Initially had start pushed back, but lingering discomfort led to move to IL out of caution.

Medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025. Has advanced to facing hitters in live BP sessions.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said Austin could miss "months" while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Caught for Caleb Ferguson as he faced hitters on April 20 and is scheduled to hit against Nick Lodolo on April 21.

Threw 16 pitches in one simulated inning vs. hitters in live BP on April 20. Is scheduled to pitch to hitters again on April 24 in Cincinnati. If that goes well, Ferguson would likely begin a rehab assignment of at least six appearances.
Threw 31 pitches in two simulated innings in batting practice on April 17. Slated to bump up to three innings on April 21.
Had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow. Shut down from throwing for six weeks; expected to miss 14-16 weeks.

114.status: Made fourth appearance of rehab assignment on April 17 with Triple-A Columbus
Suffered injury running to second base while hitting a double in fifth inning on April 6. Imaging revealed moderate strain. Expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Reported feeling pain-free and effective during an April 20 bullpen session. Will face hitters at Coors Field on April 22.
Faced hitters at Coors Field the morning of April 19 with solid velocity. A Minor League assignment is coming at some point soon.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.
Dr. Keith Meister performed Tommy John surgery with an internal brace March 20 in Dallas. Petit, a Rule 5 Draft pick from the Tigers, will miss the season.
Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Forced out of the April 15 game following a collision with Jac Caglianone at third base, There will be additional evaluations before determining his timeline for return.
Threw another bullpen session on April 17.
Received a cortisone injection in mid-April due after suffering an onset of elbow discomfort.
Threw a bullpen session April 15 that went OK but not great, according to manager A.J. Hinch. Will travel with team to Boston and continue throwing.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Underwent surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9. Feeling improvement after suffering concussion. Received five stitches to repair a laceration in mouth.
Threw a bullpen session on April 14.
Resumed throwing program on April 9 following a scheduled rest period as part of his rehab. Played catch out to 120 feet over the past few weeks.
Received PRP injection on April 9 to address shoulder soreness after throwing during the week of April 1.
Placed on 7-day injured list at Triple-A Toledo on April 5.
Underwent surgery on Feb. 2, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful.

117.status: Trammell exited against the Guardians on April 20 with the injury. He will undergo further testing, and could potentially be placed on the IL.
Manger Joe Espada said Allen will “need some time” after his back didn’t respond to treatment.
Expected to throw live batting practice on April 21 at Progressive Field.
Did some running on April 20 as he increases baseball activities.

Scheduled to pitch on April 21 for Triple-A Sugar Land. Most recently struck out all five batters he faced in an April 17 outing at Double-A Corpus Christi. He’s expected to pitch twice this week -- his final hurdle before being activated.
Scheduled to pitch for one of Houston’s Minor League affiliates on April 23.
Exited April 8 start against Rockies prior to the second inning with right shoulder tightness and will be evaluated in two weeks.

Threw his first bullpen session on April 20 since being shut down. Imaging and tests on Imai's shoulder and arm initially came back clean, so the Astros will try to focus on building up his arm strength.
Loperfido's injury, which he sustained running to first base April 17, is considered minor.
Was struck in the back by a line drive during his April 6 start, an injury that forced him to leave his April 12 start in Seattle after one inning.
Exited game against Rockies on April 8 mid-plate appearance after a swing.
Will “refrain from throwing for a few weeks,” according to the club, and will be reevaluated in two weeks. Manager Joe Espada said that the issue is muscular, with no evidence of damage to ligaments.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Has dealt with similar injury before but got worse in last week or so. Received injection to help with pain but is seeing a specialist week of April 20 to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
Has been battling valgus extension overload in elbow. Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha April 15. Threw two scoreless innings April 19 and is scheduled for another outing April 22.
Strained oblique Feb. 25. Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha April 14. Threw 4 1/3 innings April 19 and will have another outing week of April 20.
Hit by a comebacker March 28. Also has been battling an illness, which has slowed his throwing program, but threw a 15-pitch bullpen April 16.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Imaging revealed the issue after April 19 outing. Scheduled to undergo surgery on April 22.
Has had two good rehab outings with Single-A Ontario. Will likely take close to the maximum 30 days allowed to fully build up.
Participating in defensive drills, and started a swing progression on April 18, swinging in the cage. He is symptom-free.
Hitting upper 90s in his last couple bullpen sessions at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but is not ready for a rehab assignment yet.
Built up to two innings in his second live batting practice on April 15. He likely has at least two more live sessions before he can begin a rehab assignment.
Felt discomfort in his shoulder after his outing on April 12. The team will know more about his timeline after he undergoes an MRI.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Has not thrown since March 13, when he tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen before a spring start. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the time the Dodgers broke camp, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Pointed to left elbow and exited game on April 12 at Milwaukee. Was scheduled to meet with a specialist on April 20 to determine next steps.
Will undergo further testing, not throwing. Nats exercising caution because Gray is returning from Tommy John surgery.
Continuing his throwing progression. Recently began throwing bullpen sessions at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla. Threw 25-pitch bullpen on April 16.
Henry began a throwing program over the weekend, tossing 20 balls.
Threw a 15-pitch bullpen session of fastballs at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla., on April 17.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 72)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing throwing progressing, with flat ground throwing three times a week.

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 49)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Wrist soreness popped up postgame on April 14 in addition to his Achilles discomfort, which he had been dealing with since late March. No timetable or rehab progression has been announced yet.

Strained his calf running first to third on April 3 in San Francisco. Began a running progression on April 14 and did outfield drills on April 17. Expected to return during the Mets' next homestand that runs from April 21-30.
Began feeling knee discomfort during an April 12 game against the Athletics. An April 14 MRI revealed a meniscus tear, requiring surgery.

Developed a blister during an April 13 appearance in Los Angeles. Shouldn't need more than the minimum 15 days to return, though his roster spot will depend on whether the Mets have a need once he's ready.
Is about a month behind other Mets pitchers. Began a Minor League rehab assignment April 9 with Single-A St. Lucie, putting him on track to return May 9 at the latest.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Began a throwing progression April 19. No timetable for his return.

134.status: Jones will soon enter the simulation-game phase of his rehab, per Todd Tomczyk, the Pirates’ senior director of sports medicine. Tomczyk said Jones could take part in up to six simulated games, including live BP, before considering starting his rehab assignment.

Triolo “has resumed just about all baseball activities,” per Todd Tomczyk, the Pirates’ senior director of sports medicine. The infielder’s next step is to run the bases in Bradenton the week of April 20.

135.status: Has made three rehab appearances with Triple-A El Paso, including one on April 16. Needs a buildup akin to a full Spring Training, per manager Craig Stammen.
Landed on the IL after feeling "tightness" in his elbow playing catch April 10. Resumed playing light catch a few days later. No timetable for his return to a mound.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Missed World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL. Began rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso April 4 and has made two scoreless appearances.
Made one spring start, but experienced soreness afterward and was sidelined. Has resumed playing catch, but has yet to throw off a mound.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. By the end of camp, had begun doing light drills.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Exited April 17 game after tweaking the area while making a play in the field, missed next two games, then placed on IL after undergoing imaging that revealed a more severe issue.More »
Out of lineup on April 19, but issue is not expected to be serious and should return in coming days.
Suffered the injury while playing for Team Italy at World Baseball Classic. Began rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on April 8, but was transferred to 60-day IL on April 20.
Slated to begin rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on April 18 and will require the full 30-day allotment, with workload buildup beginning at two innings and/or 30 pitches.More »

Was still a few weeks away from beginning to throw at the time of GM Justin Hollander's latest update. Will remain with club in Seattle during next road trip. Suffered injury during outfielders’ drills on April 7.
Began catch play on April 16 but won't begin full buildup until late May. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 8. Suffered injury during catch play leading up to Opening Day.
Suffered the injury during brief callup from April 14-16 in San Diego, after which he underwent an MRI that revealed the issue.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Threw a scoreless inning in his first rehab appearance with Triple-A Sacramento on April 15.
Has given up four runs over 2 2/3 innings in his first three rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Broke his hamate on a swing on April 14; expected to miss six to eight weeks after undergoing surgery to remove the bone from his wrist on April 16.
Will be eligible to return on April 22 but will likely need to go on a rehab assignment before he's activated off the IL.
Began a throwing program on April 6 in Arizona. Currently throwing off flat ground.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Threw a scoreless two-thirds of an inning with two walks for Triple-A Memphis on April 18 in his second rehab outing.
Made third rehab start for Triple-A Memphis on April 14, striking out four batters over five innings of one-run baseball against Gwinnett.
Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.

139.status: Faced hitters in live batting practice on April 16 then joined Triple-A Durham to start a rehab assignment. Walked three and allowed a 442-foot homer while recording one out on April 19. Scheduled to pitch again on April 22, 24 and 26.
Sprained ankle running to first base April 11 and hasn't played on Triple-A rehab assignment since. Will be reevaluated on April 21, hoping to return to games on April 21 or 22.

After rocky relief outing for Triple-A Durham April 15 as part of rehab, Uceta reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a subscapularis strain, a new injury. Will be shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks away from returning.

"Felt it a little bit" on April 13 after throwing a 25-pitch bullpen on April 12, per manager Kevin Cash, and was transferred to the 60-day IL. Felt "unstable" after playing catch from 60 feet on April 20.
Imaging revealed no structural damage, and manager Kevin Cash called the IL move cautionary. Playing catch from 90 feet and hoping to throw a bullpen by the end of April.
Will be shut down from throwing for three to four days. Imaging revealed no serious injury, so not concerned about long-term effects. Will be built back up to cover multiple innings.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Was about three weeks away from facing hitters at that time.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Playing catch with no issues.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will resume throwing in early April, but will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.

140.status: MRI on April 19 for biceps soreness that had been bothering him since April 16. Will be evaluated in the coming days.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left Tuesday night’s loss to the A’s after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Experienced "not a setback, but he's pushed back," per manager Skip Schumaker. Threw 27 pitches in a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 2. Has not pitched since.
Suffered an intercostal strain during a perfect inning of relief on April 4. Manager Skip Schumaker said he doesn’t expect the IL stint to last too long.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Scheduled to make a rehab start April 21. Target pitch count is 70-75.
Scheduled to throw around 60 pitches in a rehab assignment for Single-A Dunedin on April 22.
Scheduled to throw live to hitters for the first time April 22.
Scheduled to throw another bullpen session in Dunedin on April 21.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery on April 7 and had a screw inserted in his thumb. Expectation is a six-week recovery.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Homered in first game of rehab assignment on April 18. Scheduled to play five innings at third base on April 19, and should still be on track to be activated when eligible.
Scratched from April 19’s lineup due to recurring discomfort after being hit by pitch on April 13.
Has been assigned to Single-A Fort Myers to begin a rehabilitation assignment.
Laweryson was unavailable April 10 versus the Blue Jays due to right forearm soreness and was placed on the IL.
Threw off a mound for the first time on April 7. Still at least six weeks away from returning.

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >

143.status: Made his fifth rehab start on April 19 and rejoined the Phillies at Wrigley Field on April 20. Club is waiting to see how his body responds before announcing any next steps.

143.playerName: Aidan Miller (Phillies' No. 1 prospect, No. 18 overall)
143.status: Miller is fielding ground balls. “He’s doing everything except for swinging,” manager Rob Thomson said. “We’re hoping to get that going this week.” Miller’s progress has been slow and the Phillies have declined to delve into details about it.
The Phillies and Duran call his oblique injury “very mild.” There’s no timetable for his return, but Phillies manager Rob Thomson hopes it’s soon after the 15 days.
He is playing catch. He felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14.
Bowlan threw from 75 feet on April 17. The Phillies think he’ll be back around April 28, when he is eligible to return from the IL.
He is throwing bullpen sessions in Clearwater, Fla. Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique.

144.status: Will likely make his second of at least three rehab starts on April 21 for Triple-A Gwinnett. Recorded three strikeouts, issued two walks and allowed just one hit over 3 1/3 scoreless innings in his first rehab start for High-A Rome on April 16.
Will play one more rehab game for High-A Rome on April 19 and then extend his rehab assignment with Triple-A Gwinnett. There is a chance he will be activated during Atlanta's next homestand.

144.injury: Torn tendon in right middle finger
144.status: Has started taking live BP and will play in a sim game on April 24. If all goes well, he could begin a rehab assignment within the next week or two.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. Could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Was with team in Sacramento before heading to Triple-A Charlotte to begin a rehab assignment on April 20. Last recovery component is running at 100 percent.
Called up from Triple-A Charlotte for bulk work in series finale against the Royals on April 12. Faced three hitters, walked all three and then left due to the injury.
Pitched in six games as of April 9 and hadn't been able to recover, according to manager Will Venable, who also said the decision to go on the IL was made quickly.
Manager Will Venable listed the injury, suffered on April 6, as a Grade 1 strain when talking on April 7. Hays will be out of action for 2 to 4 weeks.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but he was with the team for the home opener. Transferred to the 60-day IL on April 7.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.

146.playerName: Aiva Arquette (Marlins' No. 4 prospect, No. 42 overall)
146.status: Underwent core muscle surgery on Feb. 27. Sustained left groin injury during offseason workouts. Rested before arriving in Jupiter, where it flared up again.
Has played two games since beginning a rehab assignment with Triple-A Jacksonville on April 15.
Close to a rehab assignment, according to manager Clayton McCullough. Rehabbing in Jupiter, Fla. Initial timeline of 4-6 weeks after being placed on IL on March 28 (retroactive to March 25).
Will undergo left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13. Placed on IL on April 10. Felt discomfort after making diving attempt during Marlins' win over Reds on April 9.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Placed on 60-day injured list on March 25. Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 12. Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Tossed 44 pitches (36 strikes) over 4 1/3 innings to begin a Minor League rehab assignment with Double-A Somerset on April 17. Cole allowed three runs on three hits, with one walk and three strikeouts. His next outing will be with High-A Hudson Valley or Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre.

Scheduled to throw 55 to 60 pitches in live batting practice on April 18 with Double-A Somerset, then would be cleared to pitch in Minor League games. Will need approximately three rehab starts. Threw three innings and 50 pitches in live BP on April 13.

Began Double-A rehab assignment April 14. Also played April 16 and 17, and is scheduled to join Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre the week of April 20. Goal is to log 55 plate appearances and play shortstop consistently before MLB activation.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: His April 17 start was pushed into the next week with manager Pat Murphy saying the plan is a return against the Tigers in Detroit. Harrison will have a bullpen session on April 18 in Miami. (last updated: April 17)
Follow-up imaging on April 9 showed healing, and Chourio took his first swings April 17 in Miami. The Brewers project a return in early May. (last updated: April 17)
Suffered a second-degree left adductor strain in the Brewers' April 12 loss to the Nationals. (last updated: April 14)
Scheduled for two "ups" or 30-35 pitches in Arizona on April 16. If that goes well, would go on rehab assignment to Triple-A Nashville during the week of April 20. (last updated: April 14)
Was scheduled to pitch one inning in an extended Spring Training game on April 14 (last updated: April 14)

Tweaked his right knee during a relief outing against the Nationals on April 11 and reported feeling "perfectly fine" the next morning. If that remains the case, he's expected to rejoin the rotation against the Blue Jays. (last updated: April 12)
Had his stitches out on April 9 and will need several more weeks of healing before he can resume hitting. (last updated April 10)

Returned from his rehab assignment on April 4 after suffering a rib-cage injury while coming back from a shoulder injury. The setback is expected to delay his comeback by several weeks. (last updated: April 7)
Initial hope is that he can avoid surgery and recover with treatment. (last updated: April 7)
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed. (last updated: March 25)
